summary: The instance declares its ports canonically; the kit mandates THAT you declare, never WHICH. No declaration → the deploy is refused.

rules.rule: One discoverable, single-source-of-truth declaration names every long-running service and the port/range it binds.
Before deploying/starting services, verify the canonical declaration exists; absent → refuse, never default to 'some port'.
Bind or configure a port that does not appear in the canonical declaration (an invented port). Declare-then-use.
Two declared co-resident services claim the same port, or a service relies on a tool default another tool also defaults to.
